_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to examine different clause structures — including embedded clauses — that add information and expand ideas in complex sentences.

_source.w: We are learning to examine different clause structures — including embedded clauses — that add information and expand ideas in complex sentences.
y: Mastering varied clause structures gives us precise control over how we express complex ideas.
Understanding these structures helps us read and comprehend demanding academic texts.
This grammatical flexibility is a hallmark of mature, skilled writing.
eg: We analyse complex sentences with multiple embedded clauses and identify each clause's function.
We experiment with different clause positions — beginning, middle and end — to see how meaning shifts.
We use embedded clauses to add essential and non-essential information to our sentences.
We compare the effect of a complex sentence with multiple clauses versus several shorter sentences.
We identify how published authors use clause structures to control pace, emphasis and information flow.
We edit our writing to improve clarity and sophistication through varied clause structures.
